title: Whatsapp CRM Integration

posts.author: JoseV (Jose )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello.

Is there any way to integrate whatsapp and create leads in my CRM in monday from a chat automatically?
Currently, I have a Facebook Ads integration for a client where leads that fill out the form go directly to a monday dashboard, but now another facebook campaign has been launched that instead of a form, leads to a whatsapp. Is there any way to get those leads once they write in whatsapp to the monday dashboard automatically? Or any workaround to this particular case.

Thanks!

posts.author: fonadain (Amit Kumar)
posts.cookedHtml:
There are two ways to bring WhatsApp leads into your Monday.com CRM:

Native Integration: Monday.com now offers a built-in WhatsApp integration that creates leads and lets you manage chats within the platform (check their announcement: [neighborhood. monday.com]).

Third-party Tools: Instruments like StartADAM analyze chats and generate leads according to your specifications.

Remember: These might not capture entire history of chat, so you need to add further details yourself. Define easily understandable triggers (e. g. keywords etc. ) for lead creation.

If you have any other questions, please let me know.

Thanks,

Amit Kumar
Fonada

posts.author: JoseV (Jose )

posts.author: Fallindrews (Fallindrews)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve been exploring different tools myself, and I stumbled upon Planfix. They have this interesting setup for nonprofits, which could be handy if you’re working in that sector. You can check it out here: https://planfix.com/nonprofit/.

Back to WhatsApp and Monday.com - I totally get the excitement. It sounds like a game-changer, especially for customer support and keeping track of conversations. I’m curious, has anyone here tried it out yet? How smooth is the integration? Does it sync up well with all the tasks and projects on Monday.com?

Also, any tips on setting it up? Sometimes these integrations can be a bit tricky, and it’d be awesome to hear about any hiccups or cool features you’ve discovered. Let’s help each other out!

posts.author: Argonexio (Alek)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve been running my own chatbot setup and had a smooth time using ultramsg because I could plug it straight into my CRM without needing extra tools. The quick QR onboarding and no per‑message fees made testing way less stressful. If you’re sending reminders or order updates, the instant delivery and uptime helped me keep things running without worrying about limits.
